segments.text: How hast thou helped him that is without power? how savest thou the arm that hath no strength?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Dead things are formed from under the waters, and the inhabitants thereof.

footnotes: and the…: or, with the inhabitants

segments.text: Hell is naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.
He stretcheth out the north over the empty place, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He holdeth back the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud upon it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: until…: Heb. until the end of light with darkness

segments.text: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Lo, these are parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him?
But the thunder of his power who can understand?
segments.speaker: God
